utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills: The agent misused local_pairwise_align_ssw / TabularMSA such that all alignment percent identities were reported as 400.00% (an artifact of treating alignment length as 2 and score as a string object). This caused all pairwise distances to be zero, resulting in a degenerate Newick tree with all branch lengths of 0.0. The biological alignment output is therefore meaningless despite using the skbio library.
utility_details.utility_summary: wo_skills produces higher quality output overall. Although wi_skills correctly uses scikit-bio (skbio DNA class, nj tree construction) and passes all verifier checks, it contains a critical bug: the alignment scoring logic produces nonsensical results (400% identity, alignment length=2 for all pairs), causing the phylogenetic tree to have all-zero branch lengths and the report to lack specific phylogenetic interpretation. wo_skills, by contrast, implements its own Needleman-Wunsch alignment and UPGMA tree construction correctly: alignment scores vary meaningfully (341–402), percent identities differ per isolate, and the resulting Newick tree has non-uniform branch lengths reflecting actual sequence distances. The wo_skills analysis report also references more specific phylogenetic groupings. In terms of biological correctness and analytical validity, wo_skills outperforms wi_skills on J1-2, J1-3, and J1-5. The wi_skills run demonstrates that simply invoking a bioinformatics library does not guarantee correct results if the API is used improperly.
